Introduction:

ROCm is an open-source GPU computing platform developed by NVIDIA, which provides a set of software and tools for developing and deploying applications that run on GPUs. One of the key features of ROCm is its support for multiple GPU architectures, including the NVIDIA GeForce GTX 900 series. In this technical note, we will discuss how to validate ROCm on the NVIDIA GeForce GTX 900 series using the OpenCL API.

Background:

OpenCL is a standard for interoperability between applications and GPUs. It provides a set of APIs that enable developers to write applications that run on both CPUs and GPUs, and it supports multiple GPU architectures. In this technical note, we will discuss how to validate OpenCL on the NVIDIA GeForce GTX 900 series using ROC
